channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Pokémon Pokopia Bundle to Release in Europe
item.content:encoded:
A new Nintendo Switch 2 bundle is set to release in Europe, featuring a copy of the hit life-simulation title Pokémon Pokopia, it has been revealed.

Pokémon Pokopia was released to rave reviews earlier this year and is the first Pokémon title to release exclusively on the Switch 2, with Pokémon Winds & Waves set to follow.

Price rises to Nintendo Switch 2 hardware, and other consoles, have hit the industry recently, but those in Europe looking to save some money picking up the console alongside Pokémon Pokopia are set to have another option.

Switch 2 Bundle with Pokémon Pokopia Set to Release

Following on from confirmed release periods in the Americas, Australia, and New Zealand, all of which will be available from early June, Dealabs has revealed that a Switch 2 bundle coming alongside Pokémon Pokopia is also on the horizon.

Similar to other regions, the bundle is expected to be available in June, but there will be some packaging differences, as it’s stated the left-side of the box will feature a Ditto, the Pokémon that players take control of in Pokopia.

Additionally, it’s revealed the bundle will have a suggested retail price of €509, but this will only remain in effect until September 1, 2026, when price rises for the console will come into effect.

However, retailers are expected to offer discounted rates, which could reduce the price closer to €455. Again, though, expect the prices to be raised later this year.

Bundling Pokopia with a Switch 2 console makes a lot of sense, considering the staggering sales numbers and scores from critics and players alike it has received, and it could be one of the most popular items to purchase in the holiday season later this year.

Fans are eagerly awaiting to hear any news of additional content coming to Pokopia, with a growing hope for DLC, especially given that the tenth generation of the franchise won’t be released until 2027.

Is the Switch 2 Pokopia bundle a good deal? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Over 10% of All Pokémon Cards Ever Were Printed in The Last Year
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon franchise is in the midst of another boom in the 30th anniversary year, with fans struggling to get their hands on TCG product amid scalper complaints, but a staggering stat shows The Pokémon Company is doing all it can.

Social media is filled with viral clips of people fighting over products in stock, hoarding a load of items, and other shenanigans, and the general consensus is that The Pokémon Company themselves need to up production. However, the numbers show they have done exactly that.

11.7% of All Pokémon Cards Ever Were Printed in The Last Year

The Pokémon Company has released a host of stats for the fiscal year between April 2025 and March 2026, outlined by Serebii, but it’s a footnote from the website’s owner Joe Merrick that is eyewatering.

The numbers show that over 85 billion Pokémon TCG cards were printed in the last fiscal year, an increase of 10 billion compared to the year prior, and that figure is 11.7% of the total number of cards ever printed in the Pokémon TCG.

Considering the franchise is three decades old, those numbers are staggering, and it could actually be even more mindblowing in the years to come.

In December last year, The Pokémon Company’s Millennium Print Group confirmed it was the tenant of a mammoth 1.27 million square foot manufacturing campus in North Carolina, as reported by Pokebeach, with upgrades to the existing 400,000 square feet of buildings starting in early 2026.

A new state-of-the-art advanced manufacturing facility is set to be completed by 2027, and full-scale operations are expected to commence in late 2028.

This will significantly increase the printing capabilities of the Pokémon TCG even further, and it won’t be a surprise if the number of cards printed increases year on year.

So, by the time the facility is in full swing later this decade, even the staggering numbers from the past fiscal year could look tiny.

What are your thoughts on the state of the Pokémon TCG? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Who is BowieKnife99? Forza Horizon 6 Online Villain, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 has attracted millions of players worldwide, but few names have caused as much of a stir as BowieKnife99, who has been dubbed the game’s ‘online villain’.

Playground Games‘ creation is filled with Drivatars, many of which are based on real-world players and their tactics, but additional drivers are also coded in Forza Horizon 6, and BowieKnife99 is the most infamous of them all.

If you’ve seen the discourse surrounding BowieKnife99 in Forza Horizon 6 and are wondering what’s going on, Insider Gaming has the details you need.

Who is BowieKnife99 in Forza Horizon 6?

BowieKnife99 is an AI Drivatar in Forza Horizon 6 that has been wreaking havoc in the community with aggressive driving and thwarting opponents in races with regular wins.

The Drivatar has gone viral on social media, with even XBOX’s official X account taking jibes at the AI driver, and plenty of hilarious clips have been doing the rounds as players vent their frustration against it.

  • More Insider Gaming: Forza Horizon 6 Festival Playlist: Ohtani Treasure Hunt Location

BowieKnife99 has two main causes of annoyance, with either aggressive driving that causes collisions, forces drivers off the road, and causing other mayhem, coupled with incredible skin that can make them hard to beat if you don’t have the right car, or make a mistake in an event.

While many Drivatar’s in Forza Horizon 6 are formed based on real players and how they drive in the game, BowieKnife99 seems to be a Playground Games creation, coded into the game to fill out the opponents on the grid when there are not enough ‘real’ players to add in.

How to Get Rid of BowieKnife99 in Forza Horizon 6

If you’re sick to death of the sight of BowieKnife99, you can reduce the number of times they appear in your races by growing your Forza Horizon 6 friend list.

Forza Horizon 6 prioritizes the Drivatars of players on your friend list when creating the grid for events, so having more friends reduces the need for the game to step in and add a fully AI driver to the mix.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Forza Horizon 6 Wheelspin Exclusive Cars, Listed

This method is not foolproof, however, and you may still see BowieKnife99 appear in some events, but the more friends you have in Forza Horizon 6, the lower the odds of the menace making another appearance.

Failing that, you can always try to beat BowieKnife99 at its own game and drive it off the road aggressively, though be warned, as it will hit back hard.

Has BowieKnife99 been terrorising you in Forza Horizon 6? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Songs Of The Past DLC Officially Announced For The Witcher 3
item.content:encoded:
The long-rumored third expansion for The Witcher 3 has been officially announced by CD Projekt Red, with a release window of 2027. The announcement was originally planned for a REDstream tomorrow, on May 28, but it was pushed forward to today thanks to an accidental leak.

Titled Songs of the Past, the expansion will be available for PC, Xbox Series X|S and PlayStation 5, and it’s being co-developed by Fools Theory. More official information will be revealed late this Summer.

Everything We Know About Songs Of The Past

As for further details, CD Projekt Red has remained fairly vague, only teasing that the expansion will “take you to the Path with Geralt of Rivia once more.” With The Witcher 4 set to shift focus towards Ciri as the main protagonist, this expansion could mark the last time Geralt takes center stage for quite some time.

As for what the expansion could have in store for fans, various leaks have suggested that it may bridge the gap between the third and fourth games, while also taking players to the unexplored region of Zerrikania. The desert-inspired land is rumored to feature exotic wildlife and unique cultures, and potentially reveal how Ciri officially becomes a Witcher.

Rumors surrounding a third expansion have circulated for months through various leaks and insider reports, with some speculation previously pointing towards a May release window. However, now that it’s been confirmed for next year, it appears those earlier predictions may have been well off the mark.

The developers have also confirmed that the upcoming content update will bring revised PC system requirements, aiming to ensure smoother performance and better compatibility moving forward. These changes will come into effect alongside the next major update, with the new minimum requirements listed below:

The most notable change is that HDDs will no longer be supported, as SSDs offer faster load times and smoother asset streaming. Thankfully, it’s possible to revert to an older version of the game, though future updates will of course not be supported.

Will you be buying the next DLC for The Witcher 3? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For more gaming news, read about Persona 4 Revival getting an official rating in Korea, as well as the leaked LEGO Skylines game.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: How Many Chapters in 007: First Light? Complete Mission List
item.content:encoded:
007: First Light is now available worldwide, offering players an entirely new adventure with James Bond, and the mission list will have you spread across the entire world.

IO Interactive‘s modern take on James Bond’s origin story in 007: First Light has proven to be a big hit with critics, and could now enter the conversation as a Game of the Year contender.

If you’re wondering how many chapters and missions there are to work through in 007: First Light, Insider Gaming has all the details.

How Many Chapters in 007: First Light?

There are nine main missions in 007: First Light, split into separate chapters, and each features several cutscenes and major checkpoints.

Each main mission has a host of Challenges to be ticked off, which will be familiar to fans of the Hitman franchise as they involve ticking off various tasks, including areas of entry, takedown objectives, and similar tests.

  • More Insider Gaming: 007: First Light is the Highest-Rated James Bond Game in 30 Years

Missions themselves can’t be easily replayed from within your main save, however, and instead need to be reloaded at the main menu, although there are various Tacsim’s to complete from areas you have visited.

The full list of chapters is shown in the table below.

Complete 007: First Light Mission List

As mentioned above, each chapter in 007: First Light has a host of separate named missions involved, though some are just cutscene and dialogue sections.

In total, 007: First Light has 17 named sections of missions, spread across various locations in the world, including Iceland, England, Malta, and elsewhere.

  • More Insider Gaming: Complete 007: First Light Trophy List

All locations can be revisited in the Tacsim mode to take on various challenges, and the missions themselves need to be replayed by completionists if you want to tick off every challenge available.

The table below shows every mission in 007: First Light in order.

What are your early impressions of 007: First Light?  Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For May 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, May 27, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Part of the human body
  • Hint 2: Strong tissue
  • Hint 3: Found in the joints

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Vertebra
  • Synovial
  • Tissue
  • Joint
  • Meniscus
  • Connective
  • Tendon
  • Articular
  • Ligament

Today’s Contexto Answer For May 27, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Cartilage

Cartilage is a type of strong tissue found in humans in the joints and other places such as the nose, throat and ears.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 28.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
